element_introduction_order.first_appears: Stage 1 (implied by unused Stage 3 boss theme)

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Action-paced platformer with stage-based tension arcs; combat and platforming challenges punctuated by navigational breathing room

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory stage teaching basic mechanics and projectile combat
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased enemy density and environmental hazard complexity; stage-specific challenge patterns
notable_spikes: Boss encounters (indicated by unused Stage 3 boss theme suggesting boss-heavy progression)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage follows a traditional action platformer structure: navigable space populated with enemies, hazards, and resources, culminating in boss encounter. Suggests 6-stage structure based on cartridge downsizing context (compressed from 6mbit to 4mbit).

Factor 5 developed this as a 'technically impressive action game,' indicating emphasis on fluid combat mechanics, enemy AI variety, and visual spectacle over environmental puzzle-solving or exploration.

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Super Turrican SNES development context, stage structure references, unused music indicating Stage 3 boss design)
notes: Sources provide minimal level design specifics due to T3 data tier and archival focus. Element introduction order inferred from platformer genre conventions and boss-theme evidence. Spatial grammar and rhythm patterns derived from action platformer paradigm and Factor 5's technical design philosophy. Game downsizing from 6mbit to 4mbit suggests content compression but no detailed level documentation available.
